text.title: Weekly PM Async Broadcast Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Weekly PM Async Broadcast Template application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Every broadcast names a single accountable owner (handle, role+person). No 'team' / 'we' / 'us' as owner.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership. Downstream readers need a named human to chase.

rule.statement: Every decision MUST cite at least one named input artefact by path or URL. Free-form prose without a source citation is rejected.
rule.rationale: Typed inputs make the broadcast falsifiable. Untyped prose decays into opinion.

rule.statement: Broadcast header carries semver-style version and last_reviewed date. Stale broadcasts (>14 days) are flagged on read.
rule.rationale: Versioning forces refresh cadence; staleness flags prevent drift.

rule.statement: Every decision rationale references the specific input it acts on. Defensive phrases ('obviously', 'clearly') without input citations are rejected.
rule.rationale: Rationale anchored to inputs survives re-litigation. Post-hoc justification does not.

rule.statement: Every broadcast ends with a next-step block listing ≥1 named-owner action with date. Broadcasts without a next-step block are reading material, not operating tools.
rule.rationale: Without next-step the broadcast does not drive the week.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
